description: A reproduction of Norman Rockwell's 1943 painting 'Freedom of Speech', part of his Four Freedoms series, posted by the U.S. Department of Labor. The oil painting shows a man standing to speak at a meeting: a lean, weathered man in a plaid flannel shirt and a worn tan jacket with a folded document in his breast pocket, standing and looking upward, while older, seated men in suits around him look up at him. The palette is warm and muted, with browns, the man's blue plaid shirt, and blue papers in others' hands. There is no added on-image text or DOL seal in the image. The image is a fine-art painting reproduction. The tweet caption reads 'American Jobs should go to American Workers.'
notable_text: (no on-image text; tweet caption: 'American Jobs should go to American Workers.')
